input.prompt:
My test project targets net6.0 and uses MSTest 3.8.0. I want to migrate to MSTest v4.
Will my target framework still be supported, or do I need to change it?

data.content:
My test project targets net6.0 and uses MSTest 3.8.0. I want to migrate to MSTest v4.
Will my target framework still be supported, or do I need to change it?

My test project targets net6.0 and uses MSTest 3.8.0. I want to migrate to MSTest v4.
Will my target framework still be supported, or do I need to change it?

data.reasoningText: The user is asking about MSTest v4 compatibility with .NET 6.0. Let me look at the files in the current directory to understand their project setup, and also research what MSTest v4 requires.

toolResult.textResultForLlm:
Contents of https://www.nuget.org/packages/MSTest/4.0.0:
*   [README](#readme-body-tab)
*   [Frameworks](#supportedframeworks-body-tab)
*   [Dependencies](#dependencies-body-tab)
*   [Used By](#usedby-body-tab)
*   [Versions](#versions-body-tab)

MSTest is Microsoft supported Test Framework.

This package is a meta package that simplifies referencing all recommended MSTest packages.

Supported platforms:

*   .NET 4.6.2+
*   .NET 8.0+
*   .NET 8.0 Windows.18362+ (WinUI)
*   UWP 10.0.16299
*   UWP 10.0.17763 with .NET 9+

**Product**

Compatible and additional computed target framework versions.

.NET

net8.0 is compatible.  net8.0-android was computed.  net8.0-browser was computed.  net8.0-ios was computed.  net8.0-maccatalyst was computed.  net8.0-macos was computed.  net8.0-tvos was computed.  net8.0-windows was computed.  net9.0 is compatible.  net9.0-android was computed.  net9.0-browser was computed.  net9.0-ios was computed.  net9.0-maccatalyst was computed.  net9.0-macos was computed.  net9.0-tvos was computed.  net9.0-windows was computed.  net10.0 was computed.  net10.0-android was computed.  net10.0-browser was computed.  net10.0-ios was computed.  net10.0-maccatalyst was computed.  net10.0-macos was computed.  net10.0-tvos was computed.  net10.0-windows was computed. 

.NET Framework

net462 is compatible.  net463 was computed.  net47 was computed.  net471 was computed.  net472 was computed.  net48 was computed.  net481 was computed. 

Universal Windows Platform

uap10.0.16299 is compatible. 

*   #### .NETFramework 4.6.2
    
    *   [Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk](/packages/Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk/) (>= 18.0.0)
    *   [Microsoft.Testing.Extensions.CodeCoverage](/packages/Microsoft.Testing.Extensions.CodeCoverage/) (>= 18.1.0)
    *   [Microsoft.Testing.Extensions.TrxReport](/packages/Microsoft.Testing.Extensions.TrxReport/) (>= 2.0.0)
    *   [MSTest.TestAdapter](/packages/MSTest.TestAdapter/) (>= 4.0.0)
    *   [MSTest.TestFramework](/packages/MSTest.TestFramework/) (>= 4.0.0)
*   #### net8.0
    
    *   [Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk](/packages/Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk/) (>= 18.0.0)
    *   [Microsoft.Testing.Extensions.CodeCoverage](/packages/Microsoft.Testing.Extensions.CodeCoverage/) (>= 18.1.0)

*   #### net9.0

    *   [MSTest.TestFramework](/packages/MSTest.TestFramework/) (>= 4.0.0)
*   #### UAP 10.0.16299
    
    *   [Microsoft.Testing.Extensions.CodeCoverage](/packages/Microsoft.Testing.Extensions.CodeCoverage/) (>= 18.1.0)
    *   [MSTest.TestAdapter](/packages/MSTest.TestAdapter/) (>= 4.0.0)
    *   [MSTest.TestFramework](/packages/MSTest.TestFramework/) (>= 4.0.0)

### **NuGet packages** (8)

Showing the top 5 NuGet packages that depend on MSTest:

Package

Downloads

[Snapshooter.MSTest](/packages/Snapshooter.MSTest/)

MSTest Snapshooter is a flexible snapshot testing tool for .Net unit tests with MSTest. It creates and asserts snapshots (json format) within MSTest unit tests.

219.7K

[MSTestEnhancer](/packages/MSTestEnhancer/)

MSTestEnhancer helps you to write unit tests without naming any method. You can write method contract descriptions instead of writing confusing test method name when writing unit tests.

47.5K

[Calysto.UnitTesting](/packages/Calysto.UnitTesting/)

Provided as-is, no warranty of any kind.

5.8K

[Saintber.TestTools](/packages/Saintber.TestTools/)

提供測試輔助工具

2.4K

[XrmUnitTest.MSTest](/packages/XrmUnitTest.MSTest/)

Contains MS Test Provider classes for Dataverse/XRM Unit Testing Library

2.3K

BizHawk is a multi-system emulator written in C#. BizHawk provides nice features for casual gamers such as full screen, and joypad support in addition to full rerecording and debugging tools for all system cores.

2.7K

[Sergio0694/PolySharp](https://github.com/Sergio0694/PolySharp)

PolySharp provides generated, source-only polyfills for C# language features, to easily use all runtime-agnostic features downlevel. Add a reference, set your C# version to latest and have fun! 🚀

2.2K

[dotnet/android](https://github.com/dotnet/android)

.NET for Android provides open-source bindings of the Android SDK for use with .NET managed languages such as C#

2.1K

[microsoft/sbom-tool](https://github.com/microsoft/sbom-tool)

The SBOM tool is a highly scalable and enterprise ready tool to create SPDX 2.2 compatible SBOMs for any variety of artifacts.

*   [README](#readme-body-tab)
*   [Frameworks](#supportedframeworks-body-tab)
*   [Dependencies](#dependencies-body-tab)
*   [Used By](#usedby-body-tab)
*   [Versions](#versions-body-tab)

MSTest is Microsoft supported Test Framework.

This package is a meta package that simplifies referencing all recommended MSTest packages.

Supported platforms:

*   .NET 4.6.2+
*   .NET 8.0+
*   .NET 8.0 Windows.18362+ (WinUI)
*   UWP 10.0.16299
*   UWP 10.0.17763 with .NET 9+

**Product**

data.content:
**No, `net6.0` is not supported by MSTest v4.** You'll need to update your target framework.

MSTest 4.x supports:
- **.NET 8.0+** (net8.0, net9.0, etc.)
- .NET Framework 4.6.2+
- UWP 10.0.16299+

Since .NET 6 is already out of support (ended Nov 2024), the recommended move is to **upgrade to at least `net8.0`**. In your `.csproj`, you'd change:

```xml
<TargetFramework>net8.0</TargetFramework>
```

and update the MSTest version:

```xml
<PackageReference Include="MSTest" Version="4.2.2" />
```

Would you like me to make these changes for you?
data.reasoningText:
The NuGet page clearly shows that MSTest 4.x supports:
- .NET Framework 4.6.2+
- .NET 8.0+
- UWP 10.0.16299

So `net6.0` is **not** supported by MSTest v4. The user would need to upgrade to at least `net8.0`.
